chunks.page_summary: This document provides an overview of chemical fluid compatibility with Rheonics inline viscometer and density sensor materials, including Stainless Steel 316L, Hastelloy C22, and Titanium Gr. 5.
product_names: Rheonics Inline Density and Viscosity meter
Rheonics High Accuracy Inline Density and Viscosity meter
Rheonics HPHT* Density and Viscosity meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Process Viscometer and Density Meter Chemical Compatibility

Modified on: Tue, 5 Nov, 2024 at 2:20 PM

What products are involved?
Rheonics inline SRV viscometer (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) and density meters and viscometers SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/), and DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/).

What is the purpose of this article?
Provide overview of chemical fluids compatibility with the Rheonics sensor materials, such as Stainless Steel 316L and Hastelloy C22 for SRV and SRD, and Titanium Gr. 5 for DVP.

Material compatibility of Rheonics inline process sensors
Rheonics does not provide compatibility testing or analysis for customer fluids. Customers need to evaluate the wetted material of the sensor for their process fluids.

Viscometer and Density Meter Material Compatibility
Rheonics sensors measure a fluid's viscosity and density based on a balanced torsional resonator in the probe, that needs to be completely immersed in the fluid of interest. The most used wetted material of Rheonics probes is Stainless Steel 316L (1.4404). This is an austenitic chromium-nickel-molybdenum stainless steel variant that has a low carbon content, leading to a greater resistance to corrosion.
Rheonics Stainless Steel surface is compatible with most of the fluids used in the industry to control and monitor viscosity and/or density. However, certain chemical fluids will behave as strong acids or bases. In those cases, care should be taken to not expose the Stainless Steel sensor probe to those fluids and instead, look for other wetted materials offered by Rheonics.
The next table shows the materials available for Rheonics viscometers and density meters.

Table 1: Rheonics sensors wetted materials available

Rheonics Sensor | Description | Stainless Steel Material | Hastelloy Material | Titanium Material
---|---|---|---|---
SRV | Rheonics Inline Viscometer | Stainless Steel 316 & 316L. Wetted parts: 1.4401/1.4404/1.4435/1.4115. Non-wetted parts: 1.4435/1.4404/1.4115. Process connection flanges are often 1.4404. | Hastelloy C22. Wetted parts: C22 2.4607/2.4602. Non-wetted parts: 1.4435/1.4404/1.4115/2.8131. | -
SRD | Rheonics Inline Density and Viscosity meter | Stainless Steel 316 & 316L. Wetted parts: 1.4401/1.4404/1.4435/1.4115. Non-wetted parts: 1.4435/1.4404/1.4115. Process connection flanges are often 1.4404. | Hastelloy C22. Wetted parts: C22 2.4607/2.4602. Non-wetted parts: 1.4435/1.4404/1.4115/2.8131. | -
DVP | Rheonics High Accuracy Inline Density and Viscosity meter | - | - | Titanium Gr. 5. Wetted and non-wetted parts: 3.7164/3.7165.
DVM | Rheonics HPHT* Density and Viscosity meter | - | - | Titanium Gr. 5. Wetted and non-wetted parts: 3.7164/3.7165.

*HPHT: High Pressure and Temperature

Note: Hastelloy C22 material is available for flush, short, and hygienic long insertion probes of the SRV and SRD sensors.
Note: Material DIN Codes are not options. These must all be taken into account when checking material compatibility.

Next, a compatibility table is shown for some common chemical fluids. This table is for reference only. It is recommended that the user conducts controlled tests before final installation of the sensors if final application involves chemicals that are not already known to be suitable for the sensor wetted material. Consider as well that concentration levels, temperature and pressure are important parameters for chemical fluids and can lead to even more corrosive characteristics.
key_facts: This article was modified on Tuesday, November 5, 2024, at 2:20 PM.
The article provides an overview of chemical fluids compatibility with Rheonics SRV, SRD, and DVP sensor materials.
Rheonics sensors measure fluid viscosity and density using a balanced torsional resonator that must be completely immersed in the fluid.
Stainless Steel 316L (1.4404) is the most used wetted material for Rheonics probes.
Stainless Steel 316L is an austenitic chromium-nickel-molybdenum stainless steel with low carbon content, offering greater resistance to corrosion.
HPHT stands for High Pressure and Temperature.
A compatibility table (not included in this extract) is available for common chemical fluids, but it is for reference only.
warnings_notes: Rheonics does not provide compatibility testing or analysis for customer fluids; customers are responsible for evaluating the sensor's wetted material for their process fluids.
Care should be taken not to expose Stainless Steel sensor probes to strong acids or bases; other wetted materials offered by Rheonics should be considered in such cases.
Hastelloy C22 material is available for flush, short, and hygienic long insertion probes of SRV and SRD sensors.
Material DIN Codes are not options and must be taken into account when checking material compatibility.
It is recommended that users conduct controlled tests before final installation if the application involves chemicals not already known to be suitable for the sensor wetted material.
Concentration levels, temperature, and pressure are important parameters for chemical fluids and can lead to even more corrosive characteristics.

chunks.page_summary: This page presents a chemical compatibility table for Rheonics viscometers and density meters with various chemical fluids and materials.
chunks.extracted_text:
Table 2: Rheonics viscometers and density meters material chemical compatibility.

SRV and SRD models are compatible with:
  - Stainless Steel 316L (1.4404)
  - Hastelloy C22 (2.4602)
DVP and DVM models are compatible with:
  - Titanium Gr. 5 (3.7165)

Chemical Fluid Compatibility:
- Acetic Acid:
  - Stainless Steel 316L (1.4404): Good
  - Hastelloy C22 (2.4602): Good
  - Titanium Gr. 5 (3.7165): Good
- Acetone:
  - Stainless Steel 316L (1.4404): Good
  - Hastelloy C22 (2.4602): Good
  - Titanium Gr. 5 (3.7165): Good

  - Hastelloy C22 (2.4602): Good
  - Titanium Gr. 5 (3.7165): Good

  - Hastelloy C22 (2.4602): Good
  - Titanium Gr. 5 (3.7165): Good

  - Hastelloy C22 (2.4602): Good

key_facts: Rheonics provides chemical compatibility information for its viscometers and density meters.
The instruments are available with wetted parts made from Stainless Steel 316L (1.4404), Hastelloy C22 (2.4602), and Titanium Gr. 5 (3.7165).
SRV and SRD models of Rheonics instruments are compatible with Stainless Steel 316L and Hastelloy C22.
DVP and DVM models of Rheonics instruments are compatible with Titanium Gr. 5.
The table rates the chemical compatibility of 32 different fluids as 'Good' or 'Bad' for each material.
